author: balarāma dāsa
verses:
gajendra-gamane jāya, sakaruṇa diṭhe cāya,
padabhare mahī ṭalamala
matta siŋha jini’, kampa-māna medinī,
pāṣaṇḍi-gaṇa śuniyā vikala

āota avadhūta karuṇāra sindhu
preme garagara mana, kare hari⋅saṅkīrtana,
patita pāvana dīna-bandhu

huṅkāra kariyā cale, acala sacala naḍe,
preme bhāse amara⋅samāja
sahacara-gaṇa saṅge, vividha⋅khelana raṅge,
alakhite kare saba kāja

śeṣaśāyī⋅saṅkarṣaṇa, avatārī⋅nārāyaṇa,
jā̐’ra aṁśa kalāte gaṇana
kṛpā-sindhu bhakti-dātā, jagatera hitakartā,
sei rāma rohiṇī-nandana

jā̐’ra līlā lāvaṇya-dhāma, āgame nigame gāna,
jā̐’ra rūpa madanamohana
ebe akiñcana veśe, phire pa̐hu deśe deśe,
uddhāra karaye tri-bhuvana

vrajera vaidagdhī sāra, jata jata līlā āra,
pāibāre jadi thāke mana
balarāmadāse kaya, manoratha siddhi haya,
bhaja bhaja śrī-pādacaraṇa
en_translation:
He walks with the gait of a lordly elephant, gazing with compassion. The earth trembles under the weight of His steps. Conquering the pride of a mad lion, He makes the earth shake, and the atheists become distressed upon hearing His roar.

Here comes the Avadhūta, the ocean of mercy! His mind is overwhelmed with divine love, and He performs the congregational chanting of Hari. He is the purifier of the fallen and the friend of the humble.

He walks with a roar, shaking the movable and immovable. The assembly of demigods floats in love. With His companions, in the joy of various pastimes, He accomplishes all tasks unseen.

He is Lord Saṅkarṣaṇa resting on Ananta Śeṣa, the source of all incarnations, Nārāyaṇa. His parts and portions are countless. He is the ocean of mercy, the giver of devotion, and the benefactor of the world. He is Lord Rāma (Balarāma), the son of Rohiṇī.

His pastimes are an abode of beauty, sung in the Āgamas and Vedas. His form captivates even Cupid. Now, in the guise of a humble mendicant, the Lord wanders from land to land, liberating the three worlds.

He is the essence of the charm of Vraja and all other pastimes. If your mind desires to attain them, Balarāma Dāsa says: "Your desires will be fulfilled. Just worship, worship His divine lotus feet."
